_comment: SAMPLE maintenance scenario: a legislative change triggers an agent-proposed update to the published marriage-license model. The bill (H.B. 412) is HYPOTHETICAL / ILLUSTRATIVE for the demo and is labeled as such everywhere it appears. Real cites (§81-2-303) stay accurate. Maps to the schema's ChangeEntry / FieldChange concept.
title: Marriage License

trigger.label: Utah H.B. 412 — Vital Records Modernization Act
trigger.summary: Adds a mandatory public-release redaction step for sensitive identifiers and an archival transfer schedule for permanent vital records.

changes.label: Secondary Classification
changes.before: Exempt — Utah Code § 81-2-303(4): social security numbers may not be recorded on the marriage license but may be provided to DHHS or ORS for child support administration.
changes.after: Exempt — Utah Code § 81-2-303(4). NEW: the SSN and date of birth must be redacted before any public release of the record (H.B. 412 § 3).
changes.rationale: H.B. 412 § 3 adds a mandatory redaction step for sensitive identifiers prior to public release.

changes.label: Retention & Disposition
changes.after: Retain permanently by the county clerk; transfer a preservation copy to State Archives after 75 years (H.B. 412 § 5).
changes.rationale: H.B. 412 § 5 establishes an archival transfer schedule for permanent vital records.
